description: Time-to-exit and cost-to-exit planning bands derived from EU Data Act Art. 25/29 statutory envelopes. Bands are planning heuristics, not measured migration durations (DR-L10).

time_bands.label: ≤ 4 months
time_bands.basis: Within the standard statutory switching envelope: Data Act Art. 25(2)(d) 2-month max notice + Art. 25(2)(a) 30-day max transitional period + Art. 25(2)(g) 30-day min retrieval period.

time_bands.label: 4–10 months
time_bands.basis: Within the technically-unfeasible envelope: Art. 25(4) permits a duly justified alternative transitional period of up to 7 months when the standard transitional period is technically infeasible.

time_bands.label: > 10 months
time_bands.basis: Exceeds the maximum switching duration EU law contemplates even for technically unfeasible cases — re-platforming territory outside the statutory envelope.

cost_bands.label: No switching charges
cost_bands.basis: Matches Data Act Art. 29(1): zero switching charges (incl. data egress charges per Art. 2(36) definition) — the mandatory EU regime applicable from 12 Jan 2027.

cost_bands.label: Cost-based charges only
cost_bands.basis: Matches the Art. 29(2)-(3) interim regime: switching charges capped at costs directly linked to the switching process, disclosed pre-contract per Art. 29(4).

cost_bands.label: Uncapped or undisclosed charges, or early-termination penalties material to exit
cost_bands.basis: Falls outside both Art. 29 regimes; treated as a commercial barrier to exit.

ladder_tier_mapping.note: SOV-5-08-LMIC resolved tier feeds into time band determination (DR-L4).
ladder_tier_mapping.tier_A: eligible for low time band (same-stack takeover — no redeployment)
ladder_tier_mapping.tier_B: eligible for low-to-medium time band (control-plane redeployment; workload portability demonstrated)
ladder_tier_mapping.tier_C: forces at minimum medium time band (re-platforming or no documented path)

methodology_note: Bands are statutory envelopes used as planning heuristics (DR-L10). The time bands are derived from the legal maxima in Regulation (EU) 2023/2854; real exits can be faster. The CMA cloud market investigation (Appendix N, egress-fee evidence) provides empirical context for cost bands in the methodology page, but no CMA number is used as a threshold — only the Data Act statutory regimes.
